metadata.title: Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion — US & Israel Strikes on Iran
metadata.description: Comprehensive record of US and Israeli military strikes against Iran since February 28, 2026
metadata.israel_codename: Operation Roaring Lion (also: Operation Lion's Roar)
data_sources: Wikipedia — 2026 Israeli–United States strikes on Iran
Wikipedia — List of Iranian officials killed during the 2026 Iran war
Flashpoint — Escalation in the Middle East: Tracking Operation Epic Fury
CSIS — Operation Epic Fury and the Remnants of Iran's Nuclear Program
Hudson Institute — Operation Epic Fury: Iran's Declining Capabilities
JINSA — Operations Epic Fury and Roaring Lion updates
Al Jazeera live coverage and investigations
CNN live coverage and geolocated analysis
Defcon Level — Operation Epic Fury Naval Strikes
caveats: Fog of war: Many details remain unconfirmed or disputed
Casualty figures vary significantly between Iranian, US, Israeli, and independent sources
Coordinates are approximate, based on known facility locations and geolocated media
Weapons attributions are based on expert analysis of available footage and debris
This dataset is necessarily incomplete given ongoing hostilities and information blackouts
Internet blackouts in Iran have severely limited independent verification
Some strikes are reported in aggregate (e.g., '200 targets in 24 hours') without individual enumeration

total_targets_struck_by_day_14.source: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, March 13 briefing

senior_iranian_officials_killed.name: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
senior_iranian_officials_killed.title: Supreme Leader of Iran
senior_iranian_officials_killed.method: Israeli precision airstrike (approximately 50 aircraft dropped 100+ bombs on underground bunker)
senior_iranian_officials_killed.notes: CIA tracked location for months; accelerated timeline due to target of opportunity when senior officials gathered

senior_iranian_officials_killed.name: Ali Shamkhani
senior_iranian_officials_killed.title: Secretary of Iranian Security/Defense Council; Senior Adviser to Khamenei
senior_iranian_officials_killed.notes: Previously targeted and injured in Israeli strike during June 2025 Twelve-Day War; was overseeing nuclear negotiations with US

senior_iranian_officials_killed.name: Mohammad Pakpour
senior_iranian_officials_killed.title: Commander of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC)
senior_iranian_officials_killed.notes: Replaced Hossein Salami who was killed in June 2025 Twelve-Day War; described as leader of 'destruction of Israel' plan

senior_iranian_officials_killed.name: Brigadier General Aziz Nasirzadeh
senior_iranian_officials_killed.title: Defense Minister of Iran
senior_iranian_officials_killed.notes: Former air force commander; served as defense minister for ~18 months

senior_iranian_officials_killed.name: Major General Abdolrahim Mousavi
senior_iranian_officials_killed.title: Chief of Staff of Iran's Armed Forces
senior_iranian_officials_killed.notes: Appointed by Khamenei after death of predecessor Mohammad Bagheri in June 2025 war; previously commander-in-chief of Iranian Army 2017-2025; US/EU/UK/Australia sanctioned for human rights abuses

senior_iranian_officials_killed.name: Mohammad Shirazi
senior_iranian_officials_killed.title: Head of the Military Bureau of Supreme Leader since 1989

senior_iranian_officials_killed.name: Hossein Jabal Amelian
senior_iranian_officials_killed.title: Deputy Defense Minister; Chairman of the SPND Organization

senior_iranian_officials_killed.title: Head of Intelligence Directorate, Khatam al-Anbiya emergency command; Senior Intelligence Officer, Supreme Command of Iranian Forces

senior_iranian_officials_killed.name: Gholamreza Rezaeian
senior_iranian_officials_killed.title: Head of Intelligence Department of FARAJA Police

senior_iranian_officials_killed.name: Four unnamed top officials from Ministry of Intelligence
senior_iranian_officials_killed.notes: Contributing to estimated total of ~48 senior officials killed

phase_1_opening_salvo.description: Coordinated decapitation strike and air defense suppression

strikes.weapons: ~100+ bombs dropped by ~50 aircraft on underground bunker
strikes.result: Compound destroyed; Khamenei killed along with family members and senior officials
strikes.notes: Three separate gatherings of senior officials struck simultaneously; 7+ missiles confirmed striking the area

strikes.result: Hit during strikes; Pezeshkian survived

strikes.target: Sayyid al-Shuhada military complex / IRGC Navy Asif Brigade HQ and adjacent Shajareh Tayyebeh girls' school
strikes.weapons: Tomahawk cruise missile (BGM/UGM-109 TLAM) — confirmed by video analysis from Bellingcat, BBC Verify, CNN, NPR, NYT, Washington Post, and multiple munitions experts
strikes.result: School and 7 buildings in compound destroyed; triple-tap strike reported
strikes.notes: Deadliest single strike of the war in terms of civilian casualties. School was formerly part of IRGC base but walled off since 2013-2016. Pentagon internal probe points to US Tomahawk as responsible. Trump initially blamed Iran. NPR reported only 1 staffer assigned to civilian casualty mitigation after Hegseth cutbacks.
strikes.source: Multiple — see Minab school airstrike sources

strikes.target: Air defense systems and ballistic missile launchers across western and central Iran
strikes.conducted_by: Israel (IAF — ~200 aircraft, largest combat sortie in IAF history)
strikes.result: ~500 military targets struck on Day 1 by Israel alone

strikes.target: Bushehr (port city; unclear if nuclear reactor hit)
strikes.result: City struck; unclear if Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant damaged

strikes.target: Multiple locations in Tehran — University Street, Jomhouri area, Seyed Khandan
strikes.result: Missile strikes confirmed; communications impaired; near-total internet blackout per NetBlocks (54% of normal)
strikes.source: Wikipedia citing Fars News, CNN, BBC, Netblocks

strikes.target: Iranian naval vessels (9 warships on Day 1)
strikes.region: Persian Gulf / Strait of Hormuz area
strikes.result: 9 ships destroyed and sunk per Trump/White House; includes Jamaran-class corvette sunk at Chah Bahar
strikes.source: White House statement; Trump Truth Social; Defcon Level

strikes.result: Destroyed per Trump

strikes.target: Jurf al-Sakhar (Iraq) — Popular Mobilization Forces positions
strikes.city: Jurf al-Sakhar, south of Baghdad, Iraq

phase_2_continued_degradation.description: Expanded strikes against state apparatus, broadcasting, government institutions, naval assets

strikes.result: Completely destroyed — video released by Iran International

strikes.target: Islamic Republic of Iran Broadcasting (IRIB) headquarters — state broadcaster
strikes.result: Parts of facilities struck; no casualties reported by Iranian authorities
Destroyed while Assembly expected to convene to select new Supreme Leader — delayed succession process
strikes.notes: Deliberate targeting to prevent leadership continuity

strikes.result: Building destroyed
Destroyed — to prevent regime officials from fleeing

strikes.target: Near Azadi Stadium, Azadi Square, Milad Tower
strikes.result: Explosions reported in vicinity
Targeted by airstrikes per Iranian state-linked media
Destroyed
17 ships destroyed including Iran's most operational submarine (hole in side) per CENTCOM commander Admiral Brad Cooper
Damaged during airstrikes

strikes.target: IRGC Intelligence building on 22 Bahman Street
strikes.result: Building collapsed — struck with precision; only server sections hit
strikes.notes: Citizen reports: 'Before the 12-day war, this was the main data center'

strikes.target: Rayaneh Khadamat Omid Sepah (Sepah Hope Computer Services) on Haghani Highway
strikes.result: Server sections precisely struck — company had helped Bank Sepah recover data after earlier hack

strikes.target: Saudi Aramco facility at Ras Tanura (Iranian strike, not US/Israel — listed for context)
strikes.notes: This was an Iranian retaliatory strike, not a US/Israel strike. Included for context of escalation.

phase_3_systematic_degradation.description: Shift to systematic targeting of energy, logistics, C2 infrastructure, continued TEL hunting, drone pre-launch strikes

strikes.weapons: Various (air strikes, MQ-9 Reaper strikes, HIMARS)
strikes.result: Iran's missile salvos dropped ~86%; drone attacks dropped ~73% from opening rate. From ~350 missiles/day to ~50 missiles/day.
strikes.notes: MQ-9 Reapers actively hunting and striking TELs; 11 MQ-9s lost by March 11
strikes.source: Hudson Institute; CBS News; Air and Space Forces Magazine

strikes.result: Drones struck on the ground before launch — described as significant operational development (rare in modern warfare)

strikes.target: 16 Iranian mine-laying vessels near Strait of Hormuz
strikes.result: All 16 vessels destroyed
Ballistic missile launchers and drone storage facilities destroyed

strikes.weapons: GBU-57 A/B Massive Ordnance Penetrator (bunker busters) dropped by B-2 bombers
strikes.result: Underground enrichment facilities struck. DIA preliminary assessment (leaked): Iran may have moved enriched uranium stockpile before strikes; strikes set back nuclear capability by 'months'. CIA director Ratcliffe contradicted: said new info indicated 'severe damage' requiring 'years to rebuild'.
strikes.notes: Previously struck in Operation Midnight Hammer, June 2025. Iran had not significantly rehabilitated since.

strikes.result: Underground facility struck; conflicting assessments on damage (see Natanz entry). IAEA had discovered hidden highly enriched uranium in undamaged underground facility prior to strike.
Destroyed (had been struck previously in June 2025 Operation Midnight Hammer)

strikes.type: VARIOUS — ballistic missile launchers, naval vessels

strikes.result: 43 warships sunk total (per opepicfury.info tracker)

phase_4_kharg_island_and_escalation.description: Intensified operations including Kharg Island strikes; oil infrastructure threatened; continued bombardment

specific_targets: Airport control tower / runway / airport facilities
strikes.result: Military targets destroyed; oil infrastructure explicitly spared (Trump: 'for reasons of decency'). 15+ explosions reported. Trump called it 'one of the most powerful bombing raids in the History of the Middle East'
strikes.oil_infrastructure_status: Intact — but Trump threatened future strikes if Strait of Hormuz interference continues
strikes.source: CENTCOM; Trump Truth Social; CNN geolocated video; Fars News Agency; NPR; Al Jazeera; Washington Post; NBC News; Fortune

strikes.type: VARIOUS — missile launchers, defense systems, weapons production
strikes.result: Missile launchers, defense systems, and weapons production sites struck

strikes.target: Southern Tehran — central square during rally
strikes.result: Large explosion during annual state-organized pro-Palestinian rally with thousands gathered

strikes.target: Continued strikes across Tehran, Karaj, Isfahan, Tabriz
strikes.result: Ongoing bombardment; sign that 'we are not close to de-escalation' per Al Jazeera Tehran correspondent

strikes.target: 5 USAF KC-135 refueling planes at Prince Sultan Air Base
strikes.type: IRANIAN STRIKE ON US ASSET (for context)
strikes.location: Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia
strikes.notes: Iranian strike damaged but did not destroy 5 US tankers. Listed for operational context.

strikes.result: Struck from UAE territory per Iranian FM Araghchi
strikes.source: Iranian FM Araghchi via Al Jazeera

strikes.target: Iranian oil production facilities (reported Israeli strikes)
strikes.result: Reports that Israel has been striking Iranian oil production facilities

strikes.target: Marine deployment: 2,500 Marines + USS Tripoli (amphibious assault ship)
strikes.notes: Pulled from waters near Taiwan; sent to Middle East

nuclear_facility_strikes_summary.note: Nuclear facilities were previously struck in Operation Midnight Hammer (June 2025). The 2026 strikes targeted residual/peripheral capabilities.

facilities.name: Fordow Fuel Enrichment Plant
facilities.weapon_2026: GBU-57 A/B Massive Ordnance Penetrator (B-2 delivery)
facilities.assessment: Conflicting: DIA says 'months' setback; CIA says 'years to rebuild'

facilities.name: Natanz Enrichment Facility
facilities.weapon_2026: GBU-57 A/B Massive Ordnance Penetrator (B-2 delivery)
facilities.assessment: Underground facility targeted; DIA leaked report says strikes did not collapse facilities

cruise_missiles.name: Tomahawk Land Attack Missile (TLAM) BGM/UGM-109
cruise_missiles.confirmed_use: Confirmed by Gen. Dan Caine: 'First shooters at sea were Tomahawks'; confirmed in Minab school strike investigation
cruise_missiles.notes: Gen. Caine described Tomahawks as opening salvo from sea

precision_guided_munitions.name: GBU-57 A/B Massive Ordnance Penetrator (30,000 lb)

drones.name: MQ-9 Reaper
drones.source: CBS News; Air & Space Forces Magazine

drones.name: Low-cost one-way attack drones (Task Force Scorpion Strike)
drones.notes: First-ever combat use; adapted from Iranian drone designs

rocket_artillery.name: M-142 HIMARS
rocket_artillery.confirmed_use: Live-fire missions during Operation Epic Fury, CENTCOM AOR, photographed March 9

israel_weapons.munitions: 1,200+ bombs in first 24 hours; hundreds more daily
israel_weapons.cyber: Cyberattacks targeting Iranian media and phone apps with messages calling on Iranians to rise up (per WSJ)
israel_weapons.opening_sortie: ~200 fighter jets — largest combat sortie in IAF history

key_dates_timeline.event: Operation begins ~09:45 IRST. 900 strikes in first 12 hours. Khamenei killed. 40+ senior officials killed. Minab school struck (168-180 killed). 9 Iranian warships sunk. 3 US service members killed. Iran retaliates with ~420 missiles across 9 countries.

Strikes continue. Explosions near Azadi Stadium/Tower. Iran fires missiles at Israel, UAE, Qatar, Kuwait, Bahrain, Jordan, Saudi Arabia. Trump: US accepted Iranian proposal for negotiations. Ali Larijani announces interim leadership council. Hezbollah joins war. Israel strikes Lebanon.

IRGC Malek-Ashtar building in Tehran destroyed. 20 civilians killed at Niloofar Square. IRIB state broadcaster HQ struck. US embassy in Kuwait struck by Iran and closed. Drone hits RAF Akrotiri, Cyprus.
Assembly of Experts building bombed. CENTCOM: 17 Iranian ships destroyed including submarine. Total US targets: 1,700 in 72 hours. Red Crescent: 600+ killed. F-15 friendly fire incident in Kuwait.
Senate votes on War Powers Resolution (S.J.Res. 104). House votes 219-212 against effort to halt war.

Day 7. At least 1,332 killed in Iran. Israel claims 2,500 strikes and 80% air defense destroyed. Israel strikes 400+ targets in western Iran in single day. CSIS estimates $3.7B cost for first 100 hours.
Mojtaba Khamenei elected new Supreme Leader by Assembly of Experts.
New video surfaces showing Tomahawk striking near Minab school — geolocated by Bellingcat.
HIMARS live-fire missions photographed in CENTCOM AOR.
US destroys 16 Iranian mine-laying vessels near Strait of Hormuz. Iranian missile/drone rate down 86%/73% from opening.
NPR: Pentagon probe points to US Tomahawk responsible for school strike. 11 MQ-9 Reapers lost. CBS reports.
KC-135 crashes in Iraq — all 6 crew killed (3 from Ohio ANG). US death toll reaches 13.

Day 14. Hegseth briefing: 15,000+ targets struck. Large-scale precision strike on Kharg Island (90+ military targets, oil spared). Israel hits 200+ targets in 24 hours. 2,500 Marines + USS Tripoli ordered to Middle East. Iranian warships: ~43 sunk total. Pentagon briefing with SecDef and JCS Chairman.

Iran threatens to strike UAE ports. Explosion in Fujairah from intercepted Iranian drone debris hitting oil facility. Iran issues evacuation warning for 3 UAE ports. Trump: US 'destroyed 100%' of Iran's military capability. Iran Health Ministry: 1,444 killed, 18,551 injured. Hengaw: 4,400+ military killed. Ongoing strikes across Tehran, Karaj, Isfahan, Tabriz.

Trump says may hit Kharg Island again 'just for fun'. Urges allies to send warships to Strait of Hormuz. USS Nimitz deployment extended. Iran FM vows retaliation for Kharg strikes. Conflict in third week.
